ID:       ddx-ef2a7638
Title:    cli: expose DDx execution cleanup command and report
Type:     task
Status:   in_progress
Priority: 1
Labels:   phase:2, area:cli, area:agent, kind:implementation, reliability, spec:FEAT-010, spec:SD-025, cleanup, operator-ux
Owner:    erik
Parent:   ddx-f3dfde0d
Deps:     ddx-5d154c5d
Desc:     PROBLEM
Operators currently have no first-class command to inspect and clean DDx-owned stale execution resources. During the May 6 incident, cleanup required manual shell inspection of /tmp/ddx-exec-wt, git worktree list, and inode usage, which is error-prone and can accidentally delete non-DDx or preserved state.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- cli/cmd/command_factory.go:561-562 registers top-level work/try commands but there is no ddx cleanup or doctor cleanup command that invokes the execution cleanup manager.
- cli/cmd/doctor.go:111-202 checks general repo health but does not expose DDx execution temp worktree/liveness cleanup.
- cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:257-279 defines DDx temp worktree locations, but operators must discover and prune them manually.
- docs/helix/02-design/solution-designs/SD-025-task-execution-lifecycle.md:240-244 requires an explicit operator command such as ddx cleanup or ddx doctor cleanup that runs the same manager and reports what it removed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an operator command, preferably ddx cleanup unless existing CLI conventions strongly favor ddx doctor cleanup.
- Support dry-run and apply behavior if consistent with existing command style; at minimum produce a clear human summary and JSON output.
- Wire the command to the shared cleanup manager, not a separate shell/script implementation.
- Report removed registered worktrees, unregistered dirs, liveness files, warnings, and resource observations; never scan outside configured DDx roots.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add destructive broad system cleanup outside DDx-owned roots.
- Do not remove complete .ddx/runs or .ddx/executions evidence.
- Do not require the command to fix provider quota/auth/network failures.
Accept:   1. TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting verifies dry-run lists stale DDx resources and leaves them on disk.
2. TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources verifies apply mode invokes the shared cleanup manager and removes stale temp worktrees/liveness files.
3. TestCleanupCommand_JSONShape verifies JSON output includes removed counts, warnings, bytes/inodes when available, and blocked errors.
4. TestCleanupCommand_DoesNotRemovePreservedEvidence verifies preserved attempts and complete evidence bundles survive command execution.
5. The command is registered in the root CLI and documented in docs/agent-execute.md or adjacent operator docs.
6. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestCleanupCommand|TestExecutionCleanup" -count=1 passes.
7. lefthook run pre-commit passes.

	rootCmd.AddCommand(f.newSyncCommand())

	// Add prompts command group
	promptsCmd := &cobra.Command{
		Use:     "prompts",
		Short:   "Manage AI prompts",
		Aliases: []string{"prompt"},
	}
	promptsCmd.AddCommand(f.newPromptsListCommand())
	promptsCmd.AddCommand(f.newPromptsShowCommand())
	rootCmd.AddCommand(promptsCmd)
}

func (f *CommandFactory) warnIfInstalledBinaryBehindSource(cmd *cobra.Command) {
	if f.Version == "" || f.Version == "dev" {
		return
	}
	if f.Commit == "" || f.Commit == "unknown" {
		return
	}

	repoRoot := gitpkg.FindProjectRoot(f.WorkingDir)
	if repoRoot == "" || !gitpkg.IsRepository(repoRoot) {
		return
	}

	originURL, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "remote", "get-url", "origin")
	if err != nil || !isDDXOriginURL(originURL) {
		return
	}

	buildSHA, err := gitResolveCommit(repoRoot, f.Commit)
	if err != nil {
		return
	}
	headSHA, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
	if err != nil {
		return
	}

	if err := gitpkg.Command(context.Background(), repoRoot, "merge-base", "--is-ancestor", buildSHA, headSHA).Run(); err != nil {
		return
	}

	aheadCount, err := gitCommandOutput(repoRoot, "rev-list", "--count", buildSHA+".."+headSHA)
	if err != nil || aheadCount == "0" {
		return
	}

	_, _ = fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(),
		"WARNING: installed ddx is built from %s; source tree HEAD is %s (%s commits ahead). Run \"make install\" to refresh.\n",
		buildSHA, headSHA, aheadCount)

func setupCleanupCommandProject(t *testing.T) (string, string) {
	t.Helper()
	projectRoot := t.TempDir()
	tempRoot := t.TempDir()
	t.Setenv("DDX_EXEC_WT_DIR", tempRoot)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx"), 0o755))
	return projectRoot, tempRoot
}

func writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t *testing.T, root, name, projectRoot, attemptID string) string {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(root, name)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(path, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteExecutionCleanupMetadata(path, agent.ExecutionCleanupMetadata{
		ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    attemptID,
		WorktreePath: path,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(path, "scratch.txt"), []byte("stale\n"), 0o644))
	return path
}

func TestCleanupCommand_DryRunReportsWithoutDeleting(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-dryrun-20260506T154739-deadbeef", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-deadbeef")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-feedface",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: would remove 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")
	assert.Contains(t, out, "run again with --apply")
	assert.DirExists(t, stalePath)
	assert.FileExists(t, runStatePath)
}

func TestCleanupCommand_ApplyRemovesStaleDDxResources(t *testing.T) {
	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)
	stalePath := writeCleanupCommandCandidate(t, tempRoot, agent.ExecuteBeadWtPrefix+"ddx-cleanup-apply-20260506T154739-feedface", projectRoot, "20260506T154739-feedface")
	runStatePath := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", agent.RunStateFileName)
	require.NoError(t, agent.WriteRunState(projectRoot, agent.RunState{
		BeadID:       "ddx-cleanup",
		AttemptID:    "20260506T154739-live-cafebabe",
		StartedAt:    time.Now().UTC(),
		WorktreePath: filepath.Join(tempRoot, "missing-live-path"),
	}))
	evidenceDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "executions", "attempt-complete")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(root, "cleanup", "--apply")
	require.NoError(t, err)

	assert.Contains(t, out, "cleanup: removed 1 stale temp dir(s), 0 registered worktree(s), 1 run-state file(s)")

	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
}

	projectRoot, tempRoot := setupCleanupCommandProject(t)

func TestCleanupCommand_DoesNotRemovePreservedEvidence(t *testing.T) {

	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(evidenceDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "manifest.json"), []byte(`{"attempt_id":"attempt-complete"}`), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"), []byte(`{"status":"success"}`), 0o644))
	runsDir := filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", "runs", "run-complete")

	root := NewCommandFactory(projectRoot).NewRootCommand()

	assert.NoFileExists(t, stalePath)

	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(evidenceDir, "result.json"))
	assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(runsDir, "record.json"))
}

# DDx Work: Operator Guide

This is the short operator reference for the `ddx run` / `ddx try` / `ddx work`
layers. For full flags, see `ddx run --help`, `ddx try --help`, and
`ddx work --help`.

## Which command do I run?

- **`ddx work`** — the queue-drain surface. It claims ready beads, invokes
  `ddx try`, records outcomes, and applies DDx-owned retry policy.
- **`ddx try <id>`** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. It wraps
  prompt construction, evidence capture, and merge/preserve finalization around
  one or more `ddx run` invocations.
- **`ddx run`** — one agent invocation atom. It calls upstream agent `Execute`
  exactly once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower`, and optional
  passthrough constraints.

DDx owns bead orchestration, evidence, success classification, and retry policy.
The upstream agent owns harness/provider/model routing and execution.

## Power and Passthrough

Use power bounds for normal dispatch:

```bash
ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
ddx work --top-power
```

`--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough constraints only. DDx
sends them unchanged to the agent and does not validate, fallback, rewrite, or
branch on them:

```bash
ddx run --min-power 10 --provider openrouter --model qwen3.6-27b --prompt task.md
```

If hard pins make the requested power bounds unsatisfiable, DDx records the
agent's typed error and stops with operator action required. It does not remove
pins or call route preflight to choose a substitute.

## Result Statuses

Every `ddx try` attempt reports a status in the attempt record. `ddx work`
uses these statuses plus DDx-owned evidence to decide close, preserve, stop, or
eligible retry.

| Status | Meaning | Work action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Agent produced changes and finalization succeeded | Close bead with evidence |
| `already_satisfied` | Acceptance was already met | Close bead with evidence |
| `no_changes` | Agent ran but produced no diff | Leave open; retry only if policy allows |
| `land_conflict` | Merge/finalization failed | Stop as operator-action or cooldown; do not power-retry |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Post-run checks failed after a valid attempt | Retry may raise `MinPower` if policy allows |
| `execution_failed` | Agent or environment errored | Classify before retry; deterministic setup failures do not power-retry |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Bead or prompt inputs invalid | Stop; fix tracker/spec input |

## Common Operations

```bash
# Drain the current ready queue
ddx work

# Process at most one ready bead and stop
ddx work --once

# Run as a long-lived worker
ddx work --poll-interval 30s

# Debug a specific bead
ddx try <bead-id>

# Preserve the result instead of merging it back
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge

# Run one direct prompt
ddx run --min-power 10 --prompt task.md
```

## Retry Boundary

DDx may raise `MinPower` only when DDx-owned evidence shows a stronger model
could plausibly help after a valid attempt started. It must not power-retry
dirty worktrees, merge conflicts, invalid bead metadata, unresolved
dependencies, config parse errors, missing binaries, auth failures, toolchain
setup failures, or passthrough exhaustion.

`ResolveRoute` and route candidate traces are status/debug-only. Normal
`run`/`try`/`work` execution does not call route preflight and never feeds a
route decision back into `Execute`.

## Cleanup and Resource Exhaustion

`ddx try` owns the isolated worktree lifecycle for one bead attempt. It checks
the temporary worktree root and durable evidence root before claim, removes
partial worktrees from failed setup, and removes the isolated worktree after the
attempt is merged, preserved, classified, or gracefully interrupted.

`ddx work` runs cleanup at startup, after setup/finalization failures, during
long-lived polling, and during graceful shutdown. Long-lived workers also run a
background cleanup pass occasionally with jitter and a cleanup lock so parallel
workers do not all prune at once.

Cleanup is DDx-scoped and conservative. It may remove stale DDx temp
worktrees, stale liveness files, and partial setup directories. It must not
remove preserved attempts, `refs/ddx/iterations/...`, complete evidence under
`.ddx/runs` or `.ddx/executions`, active worktrees with live liveness, or
non-DDx paths.

Use `ddx cleanup` to inspect stale execution resources. The command defaults
to dry-run mode and reports what it would remove; add `--apply` when you want
it to actually delete stale DDx temp worktrees, stale run-state files, and
other DDx-owned scratch data.

Resource exhaustion is loop-fatal. If the worktree or evidence roots run out of
bytes/inodes or become unwritable, DDx runs one cleanup pass and retries the
resource check. If the roots are still unhealthy, `ddx work` stops instead of
claiming another bead. Operators should see messages in this shape:

```text
cleanup: removed 37 stale ddx worktrees, freed 14210 inodes
resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop
```

After the smoke test on commit e83cf02, D3 already landed more than expected: `resolve_tenant_handler` composes `{tenant}:{database}` into the TenantRouter slug, giving real per-pair physical isolation. What remains is the three things that are *built but not installed*.\n\n## What is already live (confirmed by smoke test)\n\n- Path-based routing: /tenants/:t/databases/:d/... routed to per-pair SQLite files via composite slug\n- GraphQL under the nested path\n- Unprefixed REST + /graphql return 404\n- HttpAxonClient in the SDK + the UI use the new paths\n- Legacy X-Axon-Database header is gone from REST\n\n## What is still NOT installed (the gap this bead closes)\n\n### 1. JWT pipeline not enforced\n\nB1 landed `auth_pipeline::jwt_verify_layer` + `JwtIssuer`. The real gateway router in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` does NOT install this layer. The server currently runs in legacy auth mode (Tailscale or --no-auth). Install the layer as an OPTIONAL middleware: if the request has an Authorization header, run the JWT pipeline; if not, fall back to legacy auth.\n\nWire point: in `build_router_with_auth` (or wherever the data-plane nested router is constructed), add `.layer(from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), jwt_verify_layer))` before the tenant_handler middleware. The layer needs access to a `JwtIssuer` and the `StorageAdapter`. Add them to the AppState struct.\n\nCreate the JwtIssuer at server startup in `serve.rs`. Read the signing key from an env var `AXON_JWT_KEY` (base64 HMAC secret); if unset, generate a random 32-byte key at startup and log a warning (dev mode). Production key management is a future concern.\n\nThe legacy auth path (crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs Identity + Role) still works for requests without an Authorization header — the JWT layer is a no-op pass-through when the header is absent. This preserves --no-auth, --guest-role, and Tailscale whois behavior exactly as today.\n\n### 2. Audit attribution not populated\n\nF1 added `AuditAttribution` as an optional field on `AuditEntry` plus the `with_attribution()` builder. No handler currently calls it. In the JWT middleware (step 1), after `ResolvedIdentity` is installed into the request extension, also expose a `current_attribution()` helper on AppState that the audit write sites can call.\n\nFind every `AuditEntry::new(...)` call site in `crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs` (there are probably 10-20) and chain `.with_attribution(current_attribution(\u0026request))` when a ResolvedIdentity is present. When absent (legacy auth), leave the attribution as None — the existing `actor: String` field still works.\n\n### 3. gRPC service.rs still reads x-axon-database\n\n`crates/axon-server/src/service.rs:26` has `const AXON_DATABASE_HEADER: \u0026str = \"x-axon-database\"` and `grpc_current_database` reads it from metadata. The gRPC service is entirely separate from the REST gateway and D3 did not touch it. Replace this header read with path-based extraction... except gRPC doesn't have URL paths in the same way. gRPC calls use service + method names, not paths. The database has to come from either:\n\n(a) A metadata field (still a header-equivalent), OR\n(b) A new field on every request proto\n\n(a) is pragmatic. Rename the metadata header from `x-axon-database` to `x-axon-tenant-database` and carry a composite value `\"{tenant}:{database}\"`. This matches the REST composite slug. The gRPC service parses it and dispatches via the same `get_or_create_any` path.\n\nAlso add `x-axon-authorization` (or reuse the standard `authorization` metadata key) for JWT credentials. Run the JWT verify pipeline in the gRPC `check_auth` step before dispatching to the handler.\n\nFor this bead, the gRPC change is small:\n- Rename the metadata constant\n- Update `grpc_current_database` to parse the composite form\n- Update the Rust + TS gRPC clients to send the composite form\n- Add JWT auth path in `check_auth`\n\n### 4. DatabaseRouter NOT swapped in (deferred — not needed right now)\n\nD2 landed `DatabaseRouter` as a new primitive, but the gateway still uses `TenantRouter::get_or_create_any(slug)`. The smoke test confirmed this composite slug works correctly. Replacing TenantRouter with DatabaseRouter is a refactor that doesn't change behavior — defer it to a later cleanup bead. Not in this bead's scope.\n\n## Scope summary\n\nThis bead does exactly three things:\n\n1. Install B1's JWT middleware in the data-plane router, optional path (Authorization header present)\n2. Populate F1's AuditAttribution at every audit write site when ResolvedIdentity is present\n3. Migrate gRPC service.rs from x-axon-database header to the composite slug + optional JWT metadata\n\n## Tests for this bead\n\n### Integration test: crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs (NEW)\n\n- create a JwtIssuer with a known key\n- mint a JWT for a default user with grants on (default, default)\n- start the in-process test server harness that gateway_test.rs uses\n- send a request with Authorization: Bearer \u003cjwt\u003e to /tenants/default/databases/default/collections\n- assert 200 (the JWT pipeline accepted + the handler saw ResolvedIdentity)\n\n### Integration test: attribution populated\n\n- send an entity-create request with a JWT\n- query the audit log\n- assert the audit entry has `attribution.user_id` set to the JWT sub\n\n### gRPC test: composite slug\n\n- send a CreateEntity gRPC request with metadata `x-axon-tenant-database: acme:orders`\n- assert routing to the acme:orders database (check the storage file exists)\n\n### Manual smoke test\n\nStart the server, issue a JWT via `/control/tenants/default/credentials`, use the HttpAxonClient with the JWT, create an entity, verify the audit log has attribution. Write a short shell script under `scripts/cutover-smoke.sh` that does this end-to-end.\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- DatabaseRouter replacement — deferred\n- OIDC federation — follow-up\n- Signing key rotation — future ADR\n- Removing legacy Identity/Role types from auth.rs — they still serve the --no-auth and Tailscale paths\n\n## Files to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/gateway.rs — install JWT parse composite, add JWT path\n- crates/axon-server/tests/cutover_jwt_test.rs — NEW\n- scripts/cutover-smoke.sh — NEW\n- Possibly sdk/typescript/src/client.ts — update gRPC metadata key if touching gRPC SDK surface\n\n## Files NOT to touch\n\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth_pipeline.rs — B1 is canonical, just consume it\n- crates/axon-server/src/auth.rs — legacy Tailscale path, out of scope for     91	
    92	## Cleanup and Resource Exhaustion
    93	
    94	`ddx try` owns the isolated worktree lifecycle for one bead attempt. It checks
    95	the temporary worktree root and durable evidence root before claim, removes
    96	partial worktrees from failed setup, and removes the isolated worktree after the
    97	attempt is merged, preserved, classified, or gracefully interrupted.
    98	
    99	`ddx work` runs cleanup at startup, after setup/finalization failures, during
   100	long-lived polling, and during graceful shutdown. Long-lived workers also run a
   101	background cleanup pass occasionally with jitter and a cleanup lock so parallel
   102	workers do not all prune at once.
   103	
   104	Cleanup is DDx-scoped and conservative. It may remove stale DDx temp
   105	worktrees, stale liveness files, and partial setup directories. It must not
   106	remove preserved attempts, `refs/ddx/iterations/...`, complete evidence under
   107	`.ddx/runs` or `.ddx/executions`, active worktrees with live liveness, or
   108	non-DDx paths.
   109	
   110	Use `ddx cleanup` to inspect stale execution resources. The command defaults
   111	to dry-run mode and reports what it would remove; add `--apply` when you want
   112	it to actually delete stale DDx temp worktrees, stale run-state files, and
   113	other DDx-owned scratch data.
   114	
   115	Resource exhaustion is loop-fatal. If the worktree or evidence roots run out of
   116	bytes/inodes or become unwritable, DDx runs one cleanup pass and retries the
   117	resource check. If the roots are still unhealthy, `ddx work` stops instead of
